pages.full_text:
s the throne 1954. It must have been an amazing feat to feed everyone – the valleys have very, very few houses. The lakes are all long & thin. Cwmiswyth*** east of Devil's Bridge
    We've come to a beautiful campsite in a narrow valley. Peter & Magda have gone off for a walk. We are next to running water & there are cows on the flat valley bottom.
    It looked & sounded really beautiful, but as it got dark, 1000s midges came out to torment us: I opened the windows in the middle of the night, but [illegible] in the morning the midges were back & Simon had to open the back to change the gas (red to blue) & they all came in again.
__Sunday June 13th.__
    Although Peter found some news of the GB/US football match for the World Cup, it wasn't until we got to a garage & asked that we found out it was a 1–1 draw. We found a church service, Matins in Welsh, warm welcome, at Tregaron. Nice craft shop.
pages.notes: Text begins mid-sentence continuing from previous page. 'Cwmiswyth' annotated in green with asterisks and 'east of Devil's Bridge' written vertically beside it — likely a marginal annotation identifying location. The spelling 'Cwmiswyth' could be 'Cwmystwyth' (standard Welsh spelling). 'GB/US football match for the World Cup' 1-1 draw — this matches the England vs USA match on 12 June 2010 (Group C, FIFA World Cup South Africa); 'Sunday June 13th' fits 2010 as June 13 was indeed a Sunday, anchoring the year to 2010.

pages.full_text:
Rain. We went for a drink at the pub [illegible] set off & had lunch in the van. We would have liked to walk. Instead we stopped for a cup of tea, dropping off Peter & Magda at Lyttleton Rd on the way so that Magda could meet Thomas & family.

__Saturday July 4th__
    Simon & Eve & Tonic went over to Trimpley. Simon borrowed a wheelchair from church & he pushed Margaret round Arley Arboretum while Helen held Tonic. Then we had tea in the van.

__Saturday July 17th.__
    10 Simon & Eve set off, dropping Tonic at [?Karsels]. We are driving to [?Thornesweathers] for Momiji's & Koroku's graduation party. On the A14 in the other direction was a huge jam. After Cambridge we went on a parallel road for lunch at Burwell (then Swaffham & [?leds]) before rejoining the A12.
    I swum with John [&] [illegible]
    Most of the Haines family were at Sea [illegible] & the Nelsons & Koroku's friends & Momiji's friends
pages.notes: The top of the right page continues the Wales/Tregaron trip in blue ink (contrasting with green on left page). 'Lyttleton Rd' is likely Lyttleton Road, possibly in Worcestershire area given the Trimpley/Arley Arboretum references (both are near Bewdley, Worcestershire). 'Trimpley' is near Bewdley. 'Arley Arboretum' is at Upper Arley, Worcestershire. The July 4th and July 17th entries appear to be separate diary entries of different trips in a non-camping context. 'Tonic' appears to be a baby/infant (held by Helen). Destination for July 17th could be 'Thornes' or a place near Cambridge; 'Thornesweathers' uncertain — possibly a surname/house name. '[?Karsels]' is uncertain reading. 'Swaffham' is in Norfolk. The route Cambridge → Burwell → Swaffham and then A12 does not fit geographically (A12 is south of Cambridge); 'Swaffham' annotation may be incorrect — could be 'Swaffham Prior' or another name. Saturday July 17th fits 2010. July 4th as a Saturday fits 2009 or 2015; context suggests 2009 or 2010.
